title: Plugin 1 Provenance Manifest — US
description: Provenance manifest written by Plugin 1's orchestrator at end of Phase 3. Captures run_id, agent provenance (which specialists ran, what they produced, hashes of outputs), web search log (every WebSearch + WebFetch call made during verification per D9), phase timing, and hashes of output files. Plugin 2 gate G19 verifies presence and integrity of this manifest. Added in v0.3.0 to close the audit finding that Plugin 1 to Plugin 2 handoff was pure filename convention with no provenance enforcement — a hand-authored memo could pass all 17 gates without Plugin 1 ever being invoked. G19 raises the bar by requiring (a) manifest file exists at the path declared in memo.json memo_metadata.manifest_ref, (b) all required fields populated, (c) verification_calls_count >= 12 per D9, (d) output file hashes match actual files on disk. Hand-authored memos can still pass with explicit --hand-authored flag but score is capped at 7.5. Not a cryptographic integrity system — a determined adversary can forge a consistent fake manifest. The defense is against lazy hand-authoring and against confusing Plugin 1-produced output with hand-authored output in a multi-analyst setting.

manifest_version.description: Manifest schema version. v0.3.0 = initial release in Sprint 2.

ticker.description: Ticker the manifest pertains to. Must match the memo's ticker.

run_id.description: UUID v4 generated at Phase 0 start. Used to disambiguate concurrent or repeat runs on the same ticker.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when manifest was written (end of Phase 3).

us_equity_research.description: Plugin 1 version at run time, e.g. '0.3.0'

us_equity_ic_rigor.description: Plugin 2 version at run time, e.g. '0.3.0'

phase_1_5.description: Optional — only present if Phase 1 refresh round was triggered.

agent_provenance.description: One entry per specialist agent dispatched. v0.3.0: minimum 15 (Phase 1: A1+A4+A5+A8+FS=5; Phase 2: A2+A3+A3-Peers+R+A6+A-Consensus=6; Phase 3: A7+Mirror+TopicForensic+Rv2=4). Mirror is skipped for non-pair-trade names — the entry should still appear with status='skipped'.

web_search_log.description: Every WebSearch + WebFetch call made during the run. Minimum 12 per D9 (verification phase requirement). Each entry hashes the response body so the verifier can detect post-hoc edits.

verification_calls_count.description: Total count of WebSearch + WebFetch calls in the verification phase only (not Phase 1-3 specialist calls). D9 minimum.

outputs_produced.description: List of structured output files Plugin 1 produced for downstream consumption by Plugin 2. Minimum 4: <ticker>_IC_memo.md, <ticker>_structured.json, <ticker>_source_tags.json, <ticker>_scenarios.json. Plus optionally <ticker>_verification_gates.json (if Plugin 2 already ran). Each file path + sha256 hash so G19 can verify hash-on-disk matches.

orchestrator_notes.description: Optional freeform notes from the orchestrator. Reserved for future use (e.g., 'Phase 1.5 triggered because A5 returned framework-only'). Not enforced by G19.

agent_id.description: Specialist identifier per phase-1/2/3 reference files.

phase.description: Which phase this agent ran in. Phase 1.5 refresh agents reuse their original phase number.

status.description: completed = ran with web tools and returned substantive output. skipped = not applicable (e.g., Mirror for non-pair-trade). failed_then_refreshed = framework-only output triggered Phase 1.5 refresh; this entry reflects post-refresh output. framework_only = returned framework without web tool usage and was NOT refreshed (rare; should be flagged in orchestrator_notes).

output_path.description: Path to the specialist's output file (typically in outputs/workpapers/). Required if status=completed or failed_then_refreshed.

output_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the specialist's output file content. Required if status=completed or failed_then_refreshed.

tool_uses_count.description: Total tool invocations the specialist made (WebSearch + WebFetch + Read + others).

web_calls_count.description: WebSearch + WebFetch invocations specifically. Used to detect framework-only runs (web_calls_count == 0 with status=completed is a flag).

tool.description: Which tool was used. EDGAR / FRED / XBRL are tracked separately because they have structured APIs distinct from generic WebSearch / WebFetch.

query_or_url.description: WebSearch query string, or WebFetch / EDGAR / FRED / XBRL URL.

response_hash.description: SHA-256 of the response body. Detects post-hoc manifest forgery.

used_by_agent.description: Which agent issued the call (matches agent_provenance.agent_id values plus 'verification' for verification-phase calls).

path.description: Path relative to the repo root, e.g. 'outputs/NVDA_IC_memo.md'.

file_type.description: Type label so G19 can verify all required artifact types are present.
